claim_boundary: Ticket 60 searches for separator coordinates for the mixed TICKET59 cylinders and transfers that discipline to RH, Goldbach, and Twin Prime. It does not prove or disprove any of the four open problems.

attempts.attempt: Transfer TICKET60's rule: when a finite boundary cylinder remains mixed, do not promote it. Search for a named separator coordinate and then prove that coordinate symbolically.

bounded_result.separator_gate: mixed height-window zero-kernel cylinders need a spectral separator before positivity can be promoted
bounded_result.candidate_theorem: Every mixed zero-kernel lift cylinder is split by a named spectral coordinate with a strict positivity rank, or yields an off-critical escape packet.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: a zero-kernel cylinder whose positivity ambiguity survives every tested separator
bounded_result.frontier_rule: A mixed finite cylinder is either evidence for a missing coordinate or a counterexample target. It is never a proof until a symbolic separator and infinite bridge are supplied.

attempts.obstruction: This is a proof-discipline transfer, not a solution. It prevents finite mixed evidence from being misread as an infinite theorem.
attempts.candidate_theorem: Every mixed zero-kernel lift cylinder is split by a named spectral coordinate with a strict positivity rank, or yields an off-critical escape packet.
attempts.next_experiment: Build the problem-specific separator ladder and reject any route that leaves mixed cylinders unexplained.
attempts.claim_boundary: No riemann proof and no certified riemann counterexample.

attempts.proof_or_counterexample_mode: separator ladder over counted low40-to-48 cylinders
attempts.attempt: Continue from TICKET59 by testing candidate coordinates that split the 58 mixed low40 cylinders. The goal is not more sampling; it is to identify the smallest coordinate family that makes the selected lift outcomes deterministic enough to become a symbolic theorem target.

mixed_cylinder_separator_audit.discarded_route: Assume low40 cylinder identity is enough to classify higher-bit outcomes. TICKET60 rejects this because 58 selected cylinders remain mixed under low40 alone.
mixed_cylinder_separator_audit.retained_route: Use the first deterministic separator as a candidate symbolic coordinate, then prove that coordinate is computable and well-founded under lift transitions instead of replay-derived.
mixed_cylinder_separator_audit.candidate_theorem: Every mixed low40 cylinder is separated by a bounded higher-coordinate signature, and that signature extends to an automaton-counted cover with no full-period nondecreasing cycle.
mixed_cylinder_separator_audit.counterexample_target: A mixed cylinder whose ambiguity survives every bounded separator short of exact high-extension identity, or a full-period replay inside a separated cylinder.
mixed_cylinder_separator_audit.proof_boundary: This separator audit is still finite and replay-derived. It can name missing coordinates and refute under-specified boundary proofs, but it does not prove Collatz and does not certify a Collatz counterexample.

attempts.obstruction: Any separator that depends on replaying to the first failure is diagnostic, not yet a non-circular proof. It must be replaced by a symbolic transition theorem or an automaton-counted cover.
attempts.candidate_theorem: Every mixed low40 cylinder is separated by a bounded higher-coordinate signature, and that signature extends to an automaton-counted cover with no full-period nondecreasing cycle.
attempts.next_experiment: Generate CO-TICKET-61 by proving the retained separator symbolically or by converting it into an automaton-counted cover over a larger cylinder family.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Collatz proof and no certified Collatz counterexample.

attempts.attempt: Transfer TICKET60's rule: when a finite boundary cylinder remains mixed, do not promote it. Search for a named separator coordinate and then prove that coordinate symbolically.

bounded_result.separator_gate: mixed residue/cutoff margin cylinders need a character/error separator before a lower bound can be promoted
bounded_result.candidate_theorem: Every mixed Goldbach margin cylinder is split by an explicit character or error-budget coordinate, or yields a cutoff-stable exceptional packet.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: a residue/cutoff cylinder whose positive-margin ambiguity survives every tested separator
bounded_result.frontier_rule: A mixed finite cylinder is either evidence for a missing coordinate or a counterexample target. It is never a proof until a symbolic separator and infinite bridge are supplied.

attempts.obstruction: This is a proof-discipline transfer, not a solution. It prevents finite mixed evidence from being misread as an infinite theorem.
attempts.candidate_theorem: Every mixed Goldbach margin cylinder is split by an explicit character or error-budget coordinate, or yields a cutoff-stable exceptional packet.
attempts.next_experiment: Build the problem-specific separator ladder and reject any route that leaves mixed cylinders unexplained.
attempts.claim_boundary: No goldbach proof and no certified goldbach counterexample.

bounded_result.separator_gate: mixed exact-gap sieve cylinders need a parity/error separator before gap-2 mass can be promoted
bounded_result.candidate_theorem: Every mixed exact-gap sieve cylinder is split by an explicit parity-leakage coordinate, or yields a stable exact-gap mass leak.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: a sieve cylinder whose exact gap-2 mass ambiguity survives every tested separator
bounded_result.frontier_rule: A mixed finite cylinder is either evidence for a missing coordinate or a counterexample target. It is never a proof until a symbolic separator and infinite bridge are supplied.
